---
# Source Card: An Update on the Diagnosis and Management of Faltering Growth and Catch-Up Growth in Young Children

## Summary
This 2024 peer-reviewed narrative review (Meyer, Ann Nutr Metab 80(Suppl 1):18-28) consolidates competing definitions and management targets for faltering growth (FG) and catch-up growth in young children. For a pediatric-CDS growth/nutrition module it supplies several candidate anthropometric decision thresholds (a >=1 z-score weight-for-age drop as the emerging consensus FG trigger; WHO <-2 z-score undernutrition cutoffs; NICE centile-space rules with a 1-centile = 0.67-z-score conversion; Gomez/Waterlow percentage-of-median criteria) plus quantitative nutrition targets (energy/protein for catch-up, ideal PE ratio, catch-up weight-velocity tables, and micronutrient reference intakes). It is a review that re-reports numbers from external sources, and it contains an internal inconsistency in how it frames the 1-z-score threshold (weight-for-height in the abstract vs weight-for-age in the body).

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) FG evaluation trigger = weight-for-age drop of 1 z-score over >=1 month, excluding first 2 weeks of life (Cooke et al.) - locator: Introduction (post-Box 2) - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8d826ede4d08b4b3255f114dd4a6654ce64498ebe401a8415da0d10d19264b89]"
- (ev_002) Abstract states the threshold as weight-for-HEIGHT (conflict) - locator: Abstract - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:35e4e5c74548a64e116e27c28c577a896ad7c2d4c777615f5b07dde699112c3b]"
- (ev_003) WHO undernutrition = <-2 z-score (underweight/stunting/wasting; labels verbatim) - locator: Box 1 - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:bb4fe8abb482a4b7d583b6b540c446a6938cd1265cf0e537d3d505e7a04fc114]"
- (ev_004) NICE 2017 centile-space rules; 1 centile space = 0.67 z-score; alt = >=1.0 weight-for-age z-score fall (2021) - locator: Box 1 - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:93219ae4457e4c77a31db2f38a151a0f7ac4b9e2caa0a941aed01c4773426f43]"
- (ev_005) Catch-up energy/protein: 10 g/kg/day -> 2.82 g/kg/day protein, 126 kcal/kg/day, PE 8.9%; ideal PE 8.9-12% - locator: Table 1 + text - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:aaac02f7bb59b104af3d9414965d3be7e2c0ec104f4254fbf94b25d0fa496a81]"
- (ev_006) Catch-up weight-velocity (Golden): 20-day, z -1 to 0 = male 4.1, female 4.4 g/kg/day - locator: Table 3 - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:583127dad288449dcdf748dfbcf35a53bbcc4e0a5f1308b5088f58f3ca7cc8b2]"
- (ev_007) Micronutrient reference intake 1-3 years: vit A 400 ug/day, vit D 10 ug/day, zinc 5 mg/day, iron 6.9 mg/day - locator: Table 2 - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:bbcad68cbfea8352154aee1ae4f059b39c91ced3e717e1cff4c8f1d9c2777d57]"
- (ev_008) Gomez (75% median weight-for-age) and Waterlow (80% median weight-for-length) legacy criteria - locator: Box 1 - quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:380025cb2e71f8a07e6c558da73c02da6e139c23658002a449569f5223e70930]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- All thresholds here are ANTHROPOMETRIC or DIETARY-REFERENCE, not laboratory/analyzer-dependent: no assay-method dependence applies. The relevant dependency is CHART/STANDARD choice - the author explicitly warns that a centile-based FG definition "would not yield the same result" across different charts (WHO vs CDC vs RCPCH), which is why z-score-based rules are proposed for universal use.
- Population bounding is uneven across points: energy/protein table is scoped to infants <1 year (FAO/WHO/UNU); catch-up velocity table is scoped to children 65-80 cm length with sex-specific rows; micronutrient table is age-banded (0-3 mo through 4-6 yr); the FG z-score triggers are only loosely age-bounded (excludes first 2 weeks of life).
- Guideline lineage: FG consensus = Cooke et al. 2023 expert opinion; NICE NG75 = 2017 (z-score alternative 2021); WHO undernutrition standards; energy/protein = FAO/WHO/UNU 2007; catch-up rates adapted from Golden 2009; micronutrient RNIs from British Nutrition Foundation. This review re-reports (does not originate) these numbers.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The <-2 z-score WHO cutoffs (ev_003) function as a scope boundary: the source distinguishes FG (dynamic weight-for-age decline) from undernutrition/wasting/oedema (static <-2 z-score), and notes that WHO wasting/oedema management guidance applies only when a child also meets WHO wasting/oedema criteria - a handoff to a different (acute-malnutrition) pathway. The review is explicit that its content is NOT a directive for wasting/oedema management. No diagnosis/treatment/dosing rule is asserted here; all values are decision-support proposals for a CDS converter, not executable orders.

## Limitations & conflicts
- CONFLICT (intra-source): the abstract defines the 1-z-score FG threshold on weight-for-HEIGHT (ev_002) while the body and the Cooke definition use weight-for-AGE (ev_001). Preserved as a conflict; not collapsed. A CDS converter must not silently pick one.
- CONFLICT (competing definitions): the source lists multiple non-equivalent FG definitions side by side (NICE centile-space by birthweight band; >=1.0 weight-for-age z-score fall; weight-deceleration crossing >2 major centile lines; conditional weight gain lowest 5%; BMI/weight-for-age/height-for-age <5th centile; Gomez 75%; Waterlow 80%) without reconciling them. These are alternatives, not a single cutoff.
- APPARENT LABELING ERROR (verbatim preserved): Box 1 lists "Stunting <-2 z-score height for height" and "Wasting <-2 z-score height-for-age," which invert the conventional anthropometric bases (stunting=height/length-for-age; wasting=weight-for-height). Quoted exactly; the error is in the source and must be flagged, not corrected, before any CDS use.
- Table numbers are ADAPTED/re-reported from external sources (FAO/WHO/UNU 2007, Golden 2009, British Nutrition Foundation, Ramsay ref 27); primary sources should be consulted before implementing any value as a rule.
- Author has declared industry honoraria/consultancy (Danone/Nutricia, Nestle Nutrition Institute, Abbott, Reckitt); this is a Nestle Nutrition Institute workshop supplement - relevant conflict-of-interest context for a narrative review.
- GAP: no age-and-sex-specific single numeric FG cutoff is provided as an executable rule; the 1-z-score trigger is time-window-dependent (>=1 month, excluding first 2 weeks) but not otherwise age-stratified in-text.
